# Compiler Reference

CachouJS turns `.cachou` single-file components into ES modules.

| Path | Role |
|------|------|
| **`@cachoujs/compiler`** (pure JS) | Default portable compiler — no Go required |
| Optional **Go** binary (`compiler.go` / `bin/cachou-compiler`) | Faster native builds when you want them |

It is intentionally **not** a full JavaScript/TypeScript parser. It supports a practical SFC subset suitable for UI components (0.4: section-aware maps, pragmatic type strip, expanded imports).

---

## Component format

A `.cachou` file has optional top-level sections, then a template:

```html
<script>
  // Component setup — runs once when the component function is called
  const [open, setOpen] = signal(false);
  const title = props.title ?? "Untitled";
</script>

<style scoped>
  :host {
    display: block;
  }
  .card {
    padding: 1rem;
  }
  .card:hover {
    outline: 1px solid teal;
  }
  :global(.theme-dark) .card {
    background: #111;
  }
  @media (min-width: 600px) {
    .card {
      padding: 1.5rem;
    }
  }
</style>

<div class="card">
  <h2>{title}</h2>
  <button onclick={() => setOpen(v => !v)}>
    {open() ? "Hide" : "Show"}
  </button>
  {open() ? html`<p>{props.body}</p>` : null}
</div>
```

### Sections

| Section | Rules |
|---------|--------|
| `<script>` | Optional. Top-level only. Inserted into the generated component function body. |
| `<style>` | Optional. Top-level only. Emitted as a sibling `.css` file. |
| `<style scoped>` | Same, but selectors are scoped with `data-c-<name>` attributes. |
| Template | Remaining markup after script/style extraction. |

Nested `<script>` / `<style>` inside the template are not treated as component sections.

---

## Template expressions

JSX-like braces become template interpolations:

```html
<span>{count()}</span>
```

compiles to:

```javascript
html`
<span>${count()}</span>
`
```

- Nested braces inside expressions are tracked (objects, arrows, etc.).
- Empty `{}` is an error.
- Unclosed `{` is an error with line:column.
- Literal braces: `{{` → `{`, `}}` → `}`.

Quoted `>` characters inside attributes are handled so tags do not terminate early:

```html
<button title={"a > b"} onclick={e => props.save(e)}>Save</button>
```

### Static templates

If the compiled template has **no** `${...}` interpolations, the compiler emits:

```javascript
return htmlStatic("...");
```

instead of a live `html` template, skipping reactive binding work.

---

## CSS scoping

With `<style scoped>`:

1. Open tags in the template receive `data-c-<component>` (from the file name).
2. Selectors are rewritten to include that attribute.
3. `:host` becomes `[data-c-…]`.
4. `:global(selector)` unwraps to `selector` (unscoped).
5. Nested `@media`, `@supports`, `@container`, `@layer` are walked and inner rules scoped.
6. `@keyframes` and similar at-rules pass through without selector rewriting.

Unscoped `<style>` writes CSS as-is (after basic validation).

---

## Generated JavaScript

Example output shape:

```javascript
// Generated by CachouJS Compiler - DO NOT EDIT
// Source: StatCard.cachou
import * as Cachou from "cachoujs";
import "./StatCard.css";

const {
  signal,
  effect,
  createRoot,
  memo,
  store,
  batch,
  onCleanup,
  onMount,
  html,
  mapArray,
  createResource,
  htmlStatic,
  Router,
  Route,
  Layout,
  Outlet,
  Link,
  navigate,
  getPath,
  getQueryParams,
  useHead
} = Cachou;

export default function StatCard(props = {}) {
  // --- Component Setup ---
  // … script section …

  // --- Render ---
  return html`…`;
}
//# sourceMappingURL=data:application/json;charset=utf-8,…
```

- Default import specifier is **`cachoujs`**.
- Override with `-runtime <module>`.
- A minimal data-URL source map points `sources` at the original filename for editor navigation.
- Always treat generated `.js`/`.css` as build artifacts (do not hand-edit).

---

## CLI

### Via npm scripts (this repo)

```bash
npm run compile
# → node scripts/run-compiler.mjs -dir demo/components -out demo/components -runtime cachoujs

npm run compiler -- -file path/to/X.cachou -out path/to
npm run compiler:build   # go build -o bin/cachou-compiler compiler.go
```

### Direct flags

```text
cachou-compiler -file <file.cachou> [-out <dir>] [-runtime cachoujs]
cachou-compiler -dir <directory>    [-out <dir>] [-runtime cachoujs]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-file` | Compile a single `.cachou` file |
| `-dir` | Walk directory for `.cachou` files |
| `-out` | Output directory (preserves relative structure for `-dir`) |
| `-runtime` | Module specifier for the framework import (default `cachoujs`) |

### Exit codes

- Compiling a directory **fails the process** if any file errors (all errors still printed).
- Missing Go and missing binary → wrapper prints install instructions.

### Binary resolution

1. `bin/cachou-compiler` if present (built for your platform)  
2. Else `go run compiler.go` from the package root  
3. `postinstall` runs `scripts/ensure-compiler.mjs` when Go is available  
4. Skip build: `CACHOU_SKIP_COMPILER_BUILD=1`

Published packages ship **source** (`compiler.go` + wrapper), not a single-OS Mach-O binary.

---

## Vite plugin

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import { cachou } from "cachoujs/vite";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    cachou({
      dirs: ["src/components"], // compiled on buildStart
      runtime: "cachoujs",      // import specifier written into generated JS
      aliasRuntime: true        // alias cachoujs → package src in monorepo/plugin context
    })
  ]
});
```

Behavior:

- Compiles all listed dirs at build start (missing dirs skipped).
- Watches `.cachou` add/change/unlink in dev; recompiles; full reload.
- Deletes generated `.js`/`.css` when the source `.cachou` is removed.

Also available as helpers:

```javascript
import { runCachouCompiler, resolveCompilerCommand } from "cachoujs/vite";
```

---

## Diagnostics

Invalid components report **file path** and **line:column** where possible:

- unclosed template expression  
- unclosed HTML tag  
- unclosed CSS block  
- empty expression  

Local verification:

```bash
node scripts/check-compiler-diagnostics.mjs
```

---

## Supported vs unsupported

### Supported

- Top-level script + style + template  
- Nested JS expressions in `{…}`  
- Scoped and global CSS with common at-rules  
- Default export component function taking `props`  
- Destructured runtime helpers listed in the generated import  

### Not supported (today)

- Full TypeScript / JSX parse  
- `<script setup>` variants or multiple scripts  
- CSS modules API beyond attribute scoping  
- Column-accurate VLQ source maps (line-oriented maps + `sourcesContent` are emitted)  
- Importing other `.cachou` files without compiling them first  

---

## Workflow recommendations

1. Keep script sections small; extract shared logic to `.js` modules imported from script if needed (manual import lines in script are passed through as source text).  
2. Prefer `scoped` styles for components.  
3. Commit `.cachou` sources; regenerate JS in CI/build (`npm run compile` / Vite plugin).  
4. Use `-runtime cachoujs` for publishable apps; only use relative paths if you have a special bundler layout.  
5. Fail the build on compiler errors (default for directory mode).  
6. Use the [VS Code / Cursor extension](../vscode-cachou/README.md) for highlighting, compile-on-save, and Problems-panel diagnostics — see [Use the VS Code extension](./how-to/use-vscode-extension.md).
